provenance.source_corpus: Manipulating aspen ( Populus tremuloides ) seedling size characteristics to improve initial establishment and growth on competitive sites

nodes.name: tall seedlings with lower root-to-shoot ratios

source_spans.text: With competing vegetation present, tall seedlings with lower root-to-shoot ratios had less height growth aboveground and reduced root egress (relative to total root mass) belowground
With competing vegetation present, tall seedlings with lower root-to-shoot ratios had less height growth aboveground
With competing vegetation present, tall seedlings with lower root-to-shoot ratios had... reduced root egress (relative to total root mass) belowground
Reducing competing vegetation before outplanting improved height growth
Competition removal prior to planting appeared to benefit the short and medium-size classes
Competition removal prior to planting appeared to benefit the short and medium-size classes, which showed greater first-year height increment
The lack of competition was likely the main driver of aspen growth response on the Mulch site
The lack of competition was likely the main driver of aspen growth response on the Mulch site
moisture stress can have as much of an impact on seedling development as competing vegetation
moisture stress can have as much of an impact on seedling development as competing vegetation
The abundance of root competition in the Grass site... diminished the benefit of a high root-to-shoot ratio

nodes.name: benefit of a high root-to-shoot ratio

source_spans.text: The abundance of root competition in the Grass site... diminished the benefit of a high root-to-shoot ratio
or the very dry conditions at the Gravel-pit diminished the benefit of a high root-to-shoot ratio
Increased root egress should also lead to better soil plant contact
Increased root egress should also lead to better soil plant contact
